prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sampled commercial begins with a completely dark/black frame, with no visible objects or text.

By the next sampled point, the scene has changed to a black-and-white shot of a person ice skating. Only the lower legs and skates are visible, shown close to the ice surface. The skater appears to be moving across an indoor or enclosed rink; boards or railings are faintly visible in the background. The person’s upper body and identity are not shown.

At the final sampled frame, the commercial cuts to a close-up product-style shot. A filled drinking glass sits prominently in the center/background, with liquid and foam or bubbles visible at the top. Large overlaid text reads “Florida” across the front of the image. On the left edge, part of a package or container is visible, but only partial lettering can be seen, so the full product name is unclear. The image remains in black and white.
